title: The Powerhouse Analytics Engine stores time-series as value-interval triples instead of value-time tuples enabling delta and interpolation queries
content: The Powerhouse Analytics Engine is a TypeScript time-series analytics system with optional GraphQL interface, designed to run anywhere (browsers, servers, CLI). Unlike typical time-series databases that store `(time, value)` tuples, it stores `(value, start, end)` intervals, enabling interpolation functions (default: constant) and first-class delta queries. `AnalyticsSeries` are the individual metric values; `AnalyticsDimension` objects decorate series with `name` and `path` information for cross-series querying and aggregation. Paths are `/`-delimited strings determining query specificity (e.g., `/atlas/mip40/MIP40c3SP61`). LOD (level of detail) controls aggregation across dimensions; granularity (total/annual/quarterly/monthly/weekly/daily/hourly) controls time aggregation. Data cannot be input through GraphQL, only through the TypeScript API.
description: Series values have start and end times (not just a point), supporting interval interpolation, running totals, and delta calculations.
